content: A curiosity-primed active learning agent memory (from 2024-2025 SOTA designs) can be structured to dynamically gate which new task-shared knowledge gets consolidated during online meta-learning, prioritizing high-uncertainty, low-redundancy memories to simultaneously reduce catastrophic forgetting and cut unnecessary memory writes. This gating mechanism leverages the active learning agent’s built-in uncertainty quantification to solve the core consolidation bottleneck of online meta-learning.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
